arms.label: Immediate 15m entry
arms.sub: the shadow's real booking · the baseline

arms.label: First-red-1h confirmation
arms.sub: wait for the next 1h candle; enter if red

arms.label: First-red-15m confirmation
arms.sub: wait for the first red 15m candle (median 1 bar)

price_evidence.note: entry price of the delayed rule vs the immediate 15m entry, same detections. For a SHORT, a LOWER entry is WORSE — less room to the -5% TP, nearer a bounce. This is the direct cause of the delay tax, and it scales with the wait.

verdict.headline: Entry delay taxes the edge — immediate 15m wins.
verdict.detail: Waiting for a red-candle confirmation loses on this window at BOTH 1h and 15m granularity (paired, significant). The next-candle COLOUR is a real selection signal (red-next beats green-next by +28.7pp), but it cannot be harvested by delaying entry — the delay costs more than the selection buys.
